summary: Large fighter decomp-matching PR for Kirby copied special-N code, focused on src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNPk.c with small header/type updates. The diff replaces many nonmatching stubs with C implementations for Pikachu/Pichu, Koopa/Giga Koopa, Link/Young Link, and Samus-related copied specials, updates two Link helper prototypes from void to bool, adds an extern direction table, and changes a Samus dat attribute field from u32 to s32. The PR body explicitly notes one unresolved register-swap issue in ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim; bot evidence reports +10360 matched-code bytes, 44 new matches, and ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim improved only to 97.36%.

key_files.role: Primary decomp target; many previously stubbed Kirby copied special-N functions are implemented or improved, with item spawns, motion-state transitions, collision handlers, and matching-oriented local/stack shaping.
evidence_refs: changed_files: ftKb_SpecialNPk.c +1019/-160, 21 hunks
diff: replaces stubs for ftKb_PkSpecialAirN_Coll, ftKb_SpecialNKp_800FA588, Link helper functions, Koopa air collision handlers, and many later functions
bot_report: ftKb_SpecialNPk unit contains many 0% to 100% new matches

key_files.role: Header surface updated for recovered function signatures and a referenced Kirby init data table.
evidence_refs: diff: ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB500 and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB5F4 prototypes changed from void to bool
diff: added extern enum_t ftKb_Init_803CB540[]

smart_moves.text: Kept unknown or address-style function names such as ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB500 while improving known local variable names like fp, da, msid, anim_frame, idx, loader_id, and item_id.
evidence_refs: diff: ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB394 result renamed to idx
diff: Link helper bodies use fp, da, loader_id, item_id while retaining address-style function names

smart_moves.text: Updated the owning header when function bodies proved bool return values, instead of hiding the recovered signature in source-local declarations.
evidence_refs: diff: ftKb_Init.h prototypes for ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB500 and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB5F4 changed to bool

smart_moves.text: Used existing gameplay/item helpers and dat attribute fields for recovered behavior, such as lb_8000B1CC, itKoopaFlame_Spawn, it_802A83E0, it_802AF1A4, ftAnim_SetAnimRate, and ftCommon ground/air helpers.
evidence_refs: diff: ftKb_SpecialNKp_800FA588 uses lb_8000B1CC, dat attrs, HSD_Randi, itKoopaFlame_Spawn
diff: Link helpers use it_802A83E0, it_802AF1A4, it_802A850C

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovered bodies prove that a helper is a status/failure predicate, update the header signature and call sites to bool rather than preserving an old void prototype.
evidence_refs: diff: ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB500 and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB5F4 changed from void prototypes to bool prototypes
diff: implemented functions return true on spawn failure or existing arrow conditions and false otherwise

decomp_lessons.lesson: For Kirby copied-special collision transitions, preserve current animation frame, select the target motion state by copied fighter kind, then call Fighter_ChangeMotionState with the appropriate ground/air transition flags.
evidence_refs: diff: ftKb_PkSpecialN_Coll and ftKb_PkSpecialAirN_Coll select Pikachu/Pichu msid and preserve fp->cur_anim_frame
diff: ftKb_KpSpecialNStart_Coll, ftKb_KpSpecialN_Coll, ftKb_KpSpecialNEnd_Coll and air variants select Koopa/Giga Koopa msid

decomp_lessons.lesson: Keep signedness aligned with recovered arithmetic semantics; a charge timing/count field may need s32 even when previous placeholder typing used u32.
evidence_refs: diff lookup: specialn_ss_frames_per_charge_level changed from u32 to s32

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK and dummy u8 local arrays in several recovered functions to shape stack layout.
evidence_refs: diff: ftKb_SpecialNKp_800FA588 has u8 _[4] and PAD_STACK(8)
diff: ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB6DC has PAD_STACK(24) and u8 _[12]
diff: ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB500 and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB5F4 use u8 _[4]
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Global standards require narrow verifier evidence for PAD_STACK/dummy locals; the bot report supports improved matching, but the slice does not include per-function objdiff rationale for each stack tactic.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjusted local declaration placement and scoped blocks, including declaring fp inside if-blocks and separating da declarations from assignment, likely to influence register or stack allocation.
evidence_refs: diff: ftKb_PkSpecialN_Coll moves Fighter* fp into the ground-check block
diff: ftKb_KpSpecialN_Anim and ftKb_KpSpecialAirN_Anim split ftKb_DatAttrs* da declaration from assignment
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The PR body mentions an unresolved regswap in ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim, so register-allocation shaping was still incomplete for at least one function.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used s32 rather than enum-typed FtMotionId in several motion-state locals.
evidence_refs: diff: FtMotionId msid replaced with s32 msid in Pikachu and Koopa collision handlers
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The slice shows the edit but does not explain whether this was semantic type recovery or codegen steering.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used data-symbol references for float constants near the end of the diff, including ftKb_Init_804D9450/804D9454/804D9458 in position offset code.
evidence_refs: diff lookup near types.h: literal offset.x/y/z assignments replaced with ftKb_Init_804D9450, ftKb_Init_804D9454, ftKb_Init_804D9458
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Data-symbol use is high-risk under loaded standards; the slice does not include section-ownership explanation beyond the bot's small matched-data improvement.

Bot report provides validation-style evidence for aggregate matching and lists many new matches, but the slice does not include per-function build logs or objdiff details for each tactic.
No human review comments are present, so reviewer intent or acceptance rationale cannot be inferred.

The bot report includes improvements in unrelated units such as tyfigupon and gr/gm objects despite only three Kirby files being changed; treat those as report side effects or global comparison noise unless separately curated.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recovered predicate helpers should update owning header prototypes
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2295, ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB500 and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB5F4 were implemented with bool return values and ftKb_Init.h was updated from void to bool for both prototypes. This is a good example of pairing body recovery with truthful header updates.
evidence_refs: diff: ftKb_Init.h prototype changes
diff: implemented bool return paths in ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB500 and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB5F4

accepted_candidate_records.title: Register/stack shaping remained local and visible in ftKb_SpecialNPk.c
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2295 uses visible matching tactics such as PAD_STACK, dummy u8 locals, declaration placement, scoped locals, and s32 motion-state locals. The PR body also names an unresolved regswap in ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim, making this slice useful for searches about register-allocation trouble in fighter matching.
evidence_refs: diff: PAD_STACK and dummy u8 locals in several functions
human_pr_text: unresolved regswap note
text_corpus: ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim only 97.36%

source_update_candidates.title: ftKb_SpecialNPk.c owns Kirby copied special-N implementations for several copied fighters
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2295 provides evidence that src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNPk.c contains Kirby copied special-N logic for Pikachu/Pichu, Koopa/Giga Koopa, Link/Young Link, and Samus-related copied specials, including collision transitions, item spawns, and animation/IASA/collision handlers.
evidence_refs: diff: function names and bodies in ftKb_SpecialNPk.c
bot_report: ftKb_SpecialNPk match list includes Link, Koopa, Samus, and related functions
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scope fact for future search/routing, but should be curated against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Header signature updates are expected when a recovered body proves a return type
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2295 is supporting evidence for the existing truthful-header practice: when a recovered function body proves bool behavior, update the owning header prototypes instead of leaving stale void declarations.
evidence_refs: diff: ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB500 and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB5F4 prototypes changed from void to bool
diff: functions implemented with bool returns
source_update_candidates.reason: This may reinforce the loaded truthful-headers standard, but it should not be promoted automatically from a single PR.

source_update_candidates.title: Track unresolved regswap notes alongside partial matches
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2295 combines an author note about an unresolved regswap in ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim with bot evidence that the function reached only 97.36%. This suggests curators may want a source-corpus note or search convention for partial matches blocked by register swaps.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: unresolved regswap note
text_corpus: ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim 97.36%
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful as a search/indexing convention; not enough evidence for a global decomp rule.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all 44 bot-reported new matches were caused solely by ftKb_SpecialNPk.c source edits; the report includes unrelated units and the slice does not explain that noise.
Do not promote PAD_STACK, dummy u8 locals, or extern float-symbol references as general style from this PR; loaded standards require narrow verifier evidence and cleanup reasoning.
Do not claim ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim was matched; the author note and bot report indicate it remained at 97.36% with an unresolved regswap.
